text: The crontabs are fine, so check whether the thing that runs them is alive: `pgrep -x cron` or `ps -ef | grep cron`. Nothing there.
cron is a daemon that must be running for any schedule to fire. It didn't come back after the maintenance window.
Start it: `sudo service cron start` (or run `sudo cron`). Confirm with `pgrep -x cron`. On a systemd host you'd also `sudo systemctl enable --now cron` so it starts on boot.
